message:
"Making biscuits." 🍪 If you've ever had a cat push their paws in and out against a soft blanket — or your lap — you know exactly the look that comes with it: half-closed eyes, total bliss.

But why do they do it? Kneading actually starts in kittenhood, when nursing babies press against their mom to help the milk flow. Many cats carry the habit into adulthood, and it becomes their way of saying "I feel safe and content right now." There's a bonus, too: cats have scent glands in their paws, so a good knead is also them quietly marking you as THEIRS.

So the next time your cat settles onto your lap and starts kneading away, take it as the compliment it is. You're home base. 💛

Does your cat knead? Tell me where their favorite biscuit-making spot is! 🐾
